title: CORRECTION: settings drawer is iframe-side only — thin client sends MENU_ACTION
body: CORRECTION to previous ui-shell packet. The settings drawer must NOT be rendered by the thin client. The pattern in this codebase is: thin client sends MENU_ACTION postMessage into the iframe, the Next.js app opens the drawer from within itself. WHAT IS ALREADY DONE (committed to dev): 1. MetaMeRuntimeClient.tsx DRAWER_ACTION_HANDLERS now includes: settings: () => setSettingsDrawerOpen(true) This mirrors the existing wallet: () => setWalletDrawerOpen(true) pattern. 2. The settings drawer already renders inside the Next.js app (absolute left-0 top-0 bottom-0 z-50, w-80, MetaMeSettingsPanel, translate-x animation). Committed on dev. WHAT LOVABLE MUST DO — thin client only: In apps/metame-runtime-shell/app/page.tsx (or SmartMenu), add a small settings sub-button in the Be tab area that calls: postShellEvent('MENU_ACTION', { action_id: 'settings' }) That is the ONLY change needed in the thin client. The existing postShellEvent at line 710 already routes MENU_ACTION messages to the iframe DRAWER_ACTION_HANDLERS. DO NOT render a drawer in the thin client. DO NOT import MetaMeSettingsPanel in the thin client. DO NOT manage settingsDrawerOpen state in the thin client. The thin client is a pass-through. All drawers render inside the iframe. The button HTML for the thin client Be tab area: This is documented in CLAUDE.md: all UI actions in the thin client must route through the iframe bridge (postShellEvent / MENU_ACTION) — the thin client does not render panels or drawers.
